text:
byJACKSON WALKER | The National Desk

TOPICS:

PARIS (TND) —Organizers of the Paris Olympics apologized Sunday after an apparent reenactment of “The Last Supper” by drag performers drew international ire.

The Friday performance came during a floating parade along the River Seine commemorating the beginning of the Olympic Games. During the parade, a group of performers dressed in drag appeared to invoke Leonardo da Vinci’s “The Last Supper,” a famous painting depicting a scene from the Gospel of John in which Jesus Christ announces one of his apostles will betray him.

Taking to X, the official Olympic Games account explainedthe scene aimed to make viewers “aware of the absurdity of violence between human beings.” Still, major figures such as House Speaker Mike Johnson, X owner Elon Musk and Kansas City Chiefs kicker Harrison Butker expressed outrage, describing the scene as a mockery of Christianity.

Paris 2024 spokesperson Anne Descamps on Sundaysaidthe scene, a collaboration with French choreographer Thomas Jolly, had not been intended to disrespect "any religious group."

“On the contrary, I think (with) Thomas Jolly, we really did try to celebrate community tolerance,” Descamps said at an International Olympic Committee news conference. “Looking at the result of the polls that we shared, we believe that this ambition was achieved. If people have taken any offense we are, of course, really, really sorry.”

JollytoldParis-based BFM Network, however, he had not intended for the scene to show any resemblance to the famous painting.

"The idea was to do a big pagan party linked to the gods of Olympus," Jolly said. "You'll never find in my work any desire to mock or denigrate anyone. I wanted a ceremony that brings people together, that reconciles, but also a ceremony that affirms our Republican values of liberty, equality and fraternity."

FollowJackson Walker on X at @_jlwalker_ for the latest trending national news. Have a news tip? Send it to jacwalker@sbgtv.com.
title: Olympics organizers apologize for alleged drag queen 'Last Supper' parody
author: JACKSON WALKER | The National Desk
description: The Friday opening ceremony performance drew international ire.
keywords: Paris Olympics,Drag queen,Last Supper,Apology,Leonardo DaVinci,Thomas Jolly,Mockery,Community tolerance
